data.title: As a plugin developer, I have deterministic contract tests and referential integrity linting that replace the removed evals
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Add contract tests that validate signal schemas (confidence signal, replan escalate, gap classification, approach decision) and referential integrity linting that verifies skill-referenced scripts, agents, and contracts exist as real files.
**Why**: The removed promptfoo evals need deterministic replacements. Contract tests validate signal schemas against their contract definitions. Referential integrity linting catches broken cross-file references before they reach review.
**Scope**:
- IN: Contract tests that validate signal schemas against contract definitions in plugins/dso/docs/contracts/
- IN: Referential integrity linting that verifies referenced scripts, agents, and contracts exist as real files
- IN: Graceful handling of renames in referential integrity checks
- IN: Pre-commit integration for the new deterministic checks
- OUT: Promptfoo removal (separate story)
- OUT: LLM-based eval replacements

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, contract tests validate signal schemas against their contract definitions and fail when schemas are violated
  ← Satisfies: "Skill/agent definition testing uses contract tests (signal schema validation)"
- When this story is complete, referential integrity linting verifies that skill-referenced scripts, agents, and contracts exist as real files and fails when references are broken
  ← Satisfies: "referential integrity linting (verify referenced scripts, agents, contracts exist as real files)"
- When this story is complete, both checks run as pre-commit hooks
  ← Satisfies: "deterministic pre-commit replacements for the removed evals"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic
  ← Satisfies: TDD done-of-done

## Considerations

- [Testing] Contract tests must cover all signal schemas documented in plugins/dso/docs/contracts/
- [Maintainability] Referential integrity linting must gracefully handle renames — false positives on renamed files would block legitimate commits
- [Reliability] Pre-commit integration must respect the ~73s timeout ceiling

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
